_meta.dna: universal(access) = simplicity(radical) x patience(infinite) x trust(earned) x reliability(always)
_meta.question_expansion_instruction: Each floor lists 7-10 seed questions. During execution, expand to 100 questions per persona by probing deeper into the domain.

name: Tower of Elders
protocol: FORECAST -> CROSS-REF -> AUDIT -> FIX -> VERIFY -> SEAL -> POSTMORTEM

questions: Can a user with 140% system font size navigate the full UI without layout breakage?
Does the product offer a light theme or respect prefers-color-scheme:light?
Is there a plain-language explanation of what 'recipe' means in the product context?
Can a non-technical user find and activate a document management recipe within 5 minutes?
Does the search bar return meaningful results or a 404 for natural-language queries?
Are all interactive elements visible and labeled without requiring hover to discover?
Does the dark theme override system high-contrast accessibility settings (WCAG 1.3.4 violation)?
Does the CLI provide a simple first-step guide when a new user types 'help'?
Is there a 'welcome' flow that explains what to do first without a man-page dump?
Can a user who understands workflows but not developer jargon complete first setup?
Is 'solace auth login --provider anthropic' explained in plain language anywhere?
Does the help output distinguish between beginner and advanced commands?
Is there a guided wizard mode for first-time CLI users?
Are error messages written for end users or for developers?
Is there a step-by-step tutorial for setting up a Gmail monitoring recipe?
Does the install process provide clear feedback on success or failure on Windows 10?
Are video tutorials aimed at non-developer users, not just team walkthroughs?
Can a patient user complete the first task within 40 minutes of first contact?
Does a silent install failure produce any user-visible diagnostic or next step?
Is the documentation written at a reading level accessible to non-technical users?
Are there visual guides with screenshots showing each step of setup?
Does the approval dialog use language appropriate for healthcare contexts (not 'execute')?
Is the word 'Deny' replaced with a less judgmental alternative like 'Decline'?
Can a user set up medication reminder scheduling without developer knowledge?
Does the scheduler interface use plain-language time descriptions?
Are healthcare-adjacent use cases (medication tracking, appointment reminders) discoverable?
Does the approval flow language avoid clinical or threatening terminology?
Is there a warm confirmation message after successful recipe execution?

floors.persona_domain: retired artist, minimal tech experience, age 78
questions: Is there a companion mode where a family member can set up and manage the product on an elder's behalf?
Does the product support family delegation (elder approves, family member configures)?
Can a WhatsApp-only user find any entry point to the product?
Is there a handoff mechanism between elder user and family helper?
Does the product work on a tablet or only on desktop?
Can the product be operated with minimal text input (voice, large buttons, simple taps)?
Is there a 'family plan' that links an elder's account to a helper's account?
Is the approval timeout configurable beyond 15 seconds?
Does the timeout provide a clear countdown visible to the user?
Can a slow reader complete the approval text before the timeout expires?
Does a timeout expiry explain what happened and what to do next?
Is the 15-second approval timeout a WCAG 2.2.1 violation (timing adjustable)?
Can the user request more time before the timeout expires?
Does the product distinguish between 'user chose to deny' and 'timeout expired'?

floors.persona_domain: former accountant, early computer adopter, age 81
questions: Is the evidence chain output presented with plain-language explanations alongside hashes?
Can a user who understands checksums verify the evidence chain independently?
Is there a narrative layer over the SHA-256 hashes translating them to plain events?
Does the evidence view show what happened in human-readable format?
Are technical terms (SHA-256, hash chain, sealed bundle) explained on first encounter?
Can a user copy evidence hashes easily for external verification?
Is the evidence chain accessible without scrolling through developer-formatted output?
Does the Mandarin (zh) locale extend beyond the homepage into the full product?
Is the CLI available in any language other than English?
Does switching to a non-English locale maintain that language throughout the entire user journey?
Are recipe descriptions available in Mandarin or only English?
Does the translation boundary (Mandarin homepage to English product) create a false promise?
Are error messages translated into the user's selected locale?
Can a Mandarin-dominant user complete setup without encountering untranslated English?
Does the Arabic locale render right-to-left text correctly in the approval dialog?
Is dir='rtl' set on the HTML element when Arabic locale is active?
Are Arabic strings free of characters from other scripts (Chinese, Cyrillic, Thai)?
Can an Arabic-dominant user read and understand the approval text before approving?
Does the product corrupt Arabic text in any dialog, notification, or display?
Are 170+ RTL CSS rules in solace-browser actually activated by JavaScript?
Does right-to-left text flow correctly in all UI components, not just the homepage?
Does the product install and run on a 5 Mbps DSL connection with a 7-year-old Windows laptop?
Are authentication error messages written in plain language, not raw stack traces?
Does local-first architecture allow core features to work offline?
Is the 'to-do list that runs itself' concept discoverable in the first 5 minutes?
Does the OAuth3 authentication failure produce a human-readable error?
Can a user with limited bandwidth complete initial setup without large downloads?
Are error messages actionable (tell the user what to do next)?
Are recipe descriptions available in Italian or only English?
Does the Italian locale extend beyond the homepage into recipe execution output?
Is the evidence chain output available in Italian?
Does the Italian translation contain correct gender agreement for all nouns?
Is the app store browsable in Italian?
Are technical terms either translated or intentionally preserved with explanations?
Does the product distinguish between marketing translation and product translation?

floors.persona_domain: retired Indian civil servant, Hindi/English, age 68
questions: Can a user share a configured recipe with their community?
Is there a shareable recipe link or community export mechanism?
Does the product support any form of community distribution?
Can an elder who successfully set up a recipe help others replicate it?
Is there a 'share what I built' feature for non-technical community members?
Does the Hindi locale work correctly for recipe execution?
Is the product designed for individual use only or does it support community adoption?

floors.persona_domain: retired Black pastor, community leader, age 77
questions: Does the product support group accounts or community mode?
Can an administrator manage what community members can access?
Is there an elder-as-administrator role for community coordination?
Does the product support multi-user access to the same recipe configuration?
Can a congregation of 300 people be served by one configured instance?
Is there a community announcement or newsletter automation recipe?
Does the product have any community coordination features beyond individual use?
Is there documented SLA for local execution reliability?
What is the guaranteed response time for recipe execution?
Is the retry policy documented for failed recipe runs?
Are failure modes documented so a methodical user can anticipate them?
Does the German locale maintain consistent formal address (Sie) throughout?
Is there a status page or reliability dashboard for the product?
Can a user see execution statistics (success rate, median time) for their recipes?
Is there a 'try without a key' path for first-time users?
Does the product require an Anthropic API key for the very first interaction?
Can an academic user explore the evidence chain without completing full setup?
Is the evidence chain mapped to academic citation concepts anywhere?
Does the free tier allow meaningful exploration before requiring payment or API keys?
Is the onboarding flow designed to let enthusiastic users reach value before hitting paywalls?
Can a user understand the product's capabilities without first setting up authentication?

floors.persona_domain: retired French social worker, empathy-first, age 67
questions: Is there a phone number, live chat, or email for human support?
Does the documentation acknowledge that users might have questions and need help?
Is the French locale tone warm and human rather than technical and transactional?
Can a user find a human contact within 15 minutes of looking?
Does the product have any human support path beyond GitHub Issues?
Is there a 'we are here if you need us' message anywhere in the product?
Can vulnerable populations be confidently referred to this product by social workers?
Does the product respect system high-contrast mode without CSS override?
At 200% browser zoom, does the layout remain usable without horizontal scrolling?
Does the approval dialog fit within the viewport at large zoom levels?
Is there a high-contrast theme option beyond the default dark theme?
Does the product pass WCAG 1.3.4 (orientation and presentation adaptability)?
Can all text be resized to 200% without loss of content or functionality?
Are color combinations meeting WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratio (4.5:1 for normal text)?
Are all audio cues paired with visual equivalents?
Does the onboarding tutorial have captions or ASL video alternatives?
Is the CLI's text-only nature recognized as an accessibility advantage for Deaf users?
Are confirmation sounds accompanied by visual indicators?
Does any critical notification rely solely on audio?
Are tutorial videos captioned in all available locales?
Is there a visual notification system for all events that would otherwise be audio-only?
Is there a caregiver mode where a trusted person manages the account?
Does the product support delegated approval (caregiver reviews, elder confirms)?
Is there a cognitive assistance mode with simplified choices?
Can actions be limited to a safe subset for users with cognitive decline?
Does the product detect and adapt to repeated confusion patterns?
Is there a 'help my elder' mode for family members supporting cognitively impaired users?
Can the approval dialog be simplified to 'Yes, do this' / 'No, don't' for cognitive accessibility?
Are all click targets at least 44x44 pixels per WCAG 2.5.5?
Is the approval dialog deny button larger than 28x28 pixels?
Can the approval dialog be operated entirely by keyboard?
Are there large-target alternatives for critical actions (approve/deny)?
Does the product support touch-forgiving interaction (not penalizing misclicks)?
Is there sufficient spacing between interactive elements to prevent accidental activation?
Can a user with motor impairment undo an accidental misclick before it takes effect?
Are all timed elements in the product configurable or extendable?
Does the 15-second approval timeout accommodate users with reduced reading speed?
Is there an 'extended time' accessibility setting for all timed interactions?
Does the session keepalive timeout allow for slow interaction patterns?
Are authentication token expiry notifications clear and non-pressuring?
Can a stroke survivor use the product without any time-pressured decisions?
Does the product comply with WCAG 2.2.1 (timing adjustable) for all timed elements?
Does the approval dialog include reassurance that actions are reversible?
Is there an 'undo' mechanism for approved actions?
Does the product offer a sandbox mode where users can explore without consequences?
Is the language in approval flows warm and non-threatening?
Does the product avoid high-stakes binary framings that could trigger anxiety?
Is there a 'what happens if I approve this?' preview showing the exact outcome?
Does the product acknowledge user hesitation without penalizing it?

floors.persona_domain: hard of hearing, hearing aids, age 71
questions: Are all audio notifications paired with persistent visual indicators?
Are tutorial videos captioned with accurate, timed subtitles?
Do hover tooltips display for longer than 0.8 seconds (at least 5 seconds)?
Are visual equivalents provided for every sound-based confirmation?
Can the product be fully operated with audio completely disabled?
Are captions available in all supported locales?
Does the product respect prefers-reduced-motion for users sensitive to animation?
Does the Korean locale render Hangul correctly without Unicode replacement characters?
Are Korean strings free of Chinese, Japanese, or other script contamination?
Can a Korean elder type a task description in Hangul and see it rendered correctly?
Does the approval dialog display Korean text without corruption?
Is the Korean locale tested with a native Korean speaker?
Are all 415 Korean locale strings free of cross-script contamination?
Does the product's non-ASCII character rendering work for all CJK scripts?
Is the Managed LLM tier value explained in terms a fixed-income elder can parse?
Can the free tier be used without understanding what an API key is?
Is there a plain-language pricing comparison showing cost vs value?
Can the product automate Medicare Explanation of Benefits monitoring?
Is the BYOK requirement explained without developer jargon?
Does the pricing page show total monthly cost without hidden fees?
Is there a 'what will this cost me?' calculator in plain language?

floors.persona_domain: elder in care facility, limited device access, age 76
questions: Can the product be installed without administrator rights on a managed Windows machine?
Does the product work through facility WiFi with content filtering and firewall restrictions?
Can the browser interface function without WebSocket connections?
Is there a web-only mode that requires no local installation?
Does local-first architecture work when the local machine is not user-controlled?
Can the product be accessed from a shared computer in a common area?
Does the product have a portable mode that runs without installation?
Can a user with IT background verify the architecture documentation against implementation?
Does the evidence chain produce verifiable output that an IT professional can audit?
Is the local-first, OAuth3, evidence-chain architecture documented in technical terms?
Can a retired IT professional install, authenticate, and run a recipe without assistance?
Does the product vocabulary (OAuth3, evidence, recipes) map to standard IT concepts?
Is the architecture documentation accurate and not aspirational?
Can the evidence chain be manually verified using standard tools (sha256sum)?

floors.persona_domain: disabled veteran, VA benefits navigation, age 73
questions: Can the approval dialog be operated entirely by voice control software?
Are all interactive elements keyboard-operable with visible focus indicators?
Does the product support voice-control compatible click targets?
Can a motorized wheelchair user with voice control complete the full approval workflow?
Is the VA benefits correspondence tracking use case supported by existing recipes?
Are focus indicators visible and meeting WCAG 2.4.7 (focus visible)?
Does the product support alternative input methods beyond mouse and keyboard?

floors.persona_domain: retired Japanese engineer, precision values, age 75
questions: Does the evidence chain provide traceability that satisfies engineering quality standards?
Are SHA-256 hashes presented in a verifiable, copyable format?
Does the product's deterministic execution model satisfy engineering reproducibility requirements?
Can a quality-control engineer verify that the same recipe produces the same result?
Is the evidence chain structure consistent with manufacturing audit trail standards?
Does the approval gate provide a clear decision record with timestamp and actor?
Is the Japanese locale correct in register (polite forms) for professional contexts?

floors.persona_domain: recently widowed, managing finances alone, age 68
questions: Does the onboarding acknowledge that the user may be managing finances for the first time?
Is there a simple financial monitoring template configurable in under 10 minutes?
Does the product greet new users with warmth rather than technical instructions?
Is there a 'set up payment tracking' recipe that requires no technical knowledge?
Does the product explain its value in terms of practical financial tasks?
Is there a guided setup for common elder financial tasks (bill tracking, deadline monitoring)?
Does the first-run experience signal 'this was made for someone like you'?

floors.persona_domain: retired Indian physician, health-system navigator, age 74
questions: Does the scheduler run recipes when the user's laptop is in sleep mode?
Is there a 'run while I sleep' option for scheduled tasks?
Can cloud execution be configured without technical setup complexity?
Does the product notify the user when a scheduled task failed because the machine was off?
Is local-first architecture reliable for scheduled tasks requiring 24/7 availability?
Can a retired physician set up health portal monitoring without a developer's help?
Does the product explain the local-vs-cloud execution tradeoff in plain language?
Does the BYOK model show exact token costs for each recipe execution?
Are there no surprise charges or hidden costs in any pricing tier?
Does the evidence chain show the user exactly what the software did and when?
Is the approval gate clearly showing every action before it runs?
Does the product provide a cost breakdown per recipe execution?
Is the local-first data privacy guarantee communicated clearly?
Can a cost-conscious user verify they are only paying for what they use?
Can a user verify that data stays on their local machine and is not sent to cloud?
Does the product provide GDPR-specific documentation for privacy-conscious users?
Are tokens revocable at any time with immediate effect?
Does the approval gate prevent any silent action without user consent?
Is there a plain-language privacy guarantee on the product's user-facing surfaces?
Can a privacy absolutist verify the product's privacy claims against the code?
Does the product provide evidence that no data leaves the local machine without consent?
Is there a family account mode where one person manages and others view?
Does the product support family delegation (one admin, multiple viewers)?
Can a family coordinator share scheduling across multiple time zones?
Is there a 'Comfort manages, children can view' access model?
Does the product support multi-user document sharing?
Can family reminder distribution be automated across 7 family members?
Is there a family hub feature set or is the product strictly individual?
Does the evidence chain output meet Swiss precision engineering standards for traceability?
Can a retired precision engineer validate the implementation against the specification?
Is the product's architecture documentation complete enough for independent verification?
Does the dark theme affect readability for an elder engineer (suboptimal but tolerable)?
Can all jargon terms be translated through engineering domain knowledge?
Is the evidence chain described as 'proper engineering' by domain experts?
Does the product's correctness, traceability, and evidence satisfy engineering values?
Can a person who has never used software beyond a basic laptop navigate the product?
Is the product's visual design welcoming to an 85-year-old who prefers paper?
Does the product provide any paper-like interface metaphor?
Can Mildred accomplish any task without calling her daughter for help?
Is there a printed quick-start guide or similar analog onboarding aid?
Does the product's first screen communicate 'this is for you' to a non-digital user?
Is the cursor-blinking terminal a barrier that prevents any engagement?
Is Persian/Farsi among the supported locales?
Can a bilingual community leader share recipes with Persian-only community members?
Does the product support a bilingual calendar (Gregorian + Solar Hijri)?
Is there a locale fallback chain for unsupported languages (fa -> ar)?
Can community information be distributed bilingually through the product?
Does the 13-locale list include Farsi, or are 80+ million speakers excluded?
Can the product handle bilingual content (English + Persian) in the same interface?

floors.persona_domain: retired social worker, suspicious of tech, age 67
questions: Is the product's privacy story told in plain language on the homepage?
Can a suspicious user find proof that the product is not surveillance within 5 minutes?
Is there a human who can answer 'what do you do with my data: nothing'?
Are privacy claims buried in technical documentation or visible on user-facing surfaces?
Does the product provide a plain-language data handling statement?
Can a social worker confidently recommend this product to vulnerable populations?
Is there a trust-building path that does not require reading technical architecture docs?

floors.persona_domain: Japanese elder, ritual and precision, age 78
questions: Does the YinYang approval gate support intentional, visible, and reversible actions?
Is there an 'undo last action' feature for fully reversible workflows?
Does the approval flow map to a mental model of intentional action (nemawashi/hanko)?
Is the dark theme replaceable with warm, light backgrounds?
Can every action be reviewed before it executes, without time pressure?
Does the Japanese locale use correct polite forms (desu/masu) throughout?
Does the product support a deliberate workflow where each step is approved individually?
Does the product work on a tablet or is it desktop-only?
Is there a WhatsApp integration for community coordination use cases?
Can an elder who coordinates church outreach set up newsletter automation?
Is setup documentation available in Spanish?
Does the product live in the environment where community elders operate (mobile/WhatsApp)?
Can a church newsletter recipe be configured from a tablet?
Is the product's value proposition communicated for community coordination use cases?
Does the recipe library contain use cases compelling to someone who can write cron jobs?
Can the product explain why it is better than a Python script for a technical elder?
Is there a value proposition that differentiates from DIY automation?
Does the product offer capabilities that are genuinely difficult to replicate with scripts?
Are there recipes for personal use cases that a 69-year-old programmer would find valuable?
Does the evidence chain provide value beyond what a log file would give?
Can a skeptical technical user find a 'killer feature' in the first session?
Does the product show aggregate execution statistics (error rate, median time, reliability)?
Is there a self-reporting dashboard for recipe performance metrics?
Can a data-driven user make an informed adoption decision based on quantified performance?
Does the evidence chain provide aggregate analytics, not just individual events?
Is there a reliability score or SLA visible to the user?
Can execution history be exported for independent statistical analysis?
Does the product quantify its own performance in terms a statistician would trust?

floors.persona_domain: retired civil rights lawyer, rights-aware, age 71
questions: Does local-first architecture genuinely prevent surveillance?
Are OAuth3 scopes genuinely preventing silent permission extension?
Do evidence chains provide transparent records of all actions?
Do approval gates provide informed consent for every consequential action?
Is there a plain-language civil rights statement in the product documentation?
Does the product know WHY its architecture is a civil rights implementation?
Can a civil rights lawyer cite the product's privacy guarantees in legal proceedings?
Does the recipe library have a metadata schema with subject headings?
Is there a controlled vocabulary for recipe categories?
Does the evidence chain function as a provenance record with catalog standards?
Is the approval gate mappable to a library access control concept?
Does the recipe catalog support search, filter, and sort by metadata?
Is there a taxonomy for organizing the recipe library at scale?
Does the product's information architecture follow library science principles?
Can a systematic, patient user complete full setup by reading every error and looking up terms?
Does the product reward determination with eventual success?
Is the Vietnamese locale the deepest and most culturally thoughtful (Tet, Father's Day)?
Can a small business email monitoring recipe be configured and run successfully?
Does the product require Vinh-level resilience to reach 8/10, or is that accessible by design?
Is the onboarding friction a design choice or an unintended barrier?
Should the product rely on elder resilience or earn it through good design?
Is the evidence chain presented in a way a non-technical judge could understand?
Can the evidence be translated into plain-language event descriptions for court use?
Is a SHA-256 hash accompanied by a narrative explaining what it witnesses?
Could the evidence chain be presented as a legal chain of custody?
Does the evidence meet judicial standards for admissibility?
Is there a human-readable evidence report export format?
Can the evidence chain be independently verified by a third party without technical expertise?
Does the product articulate its own philosophy in plain language?
Can a philosopher identify what the product is FOR and whether it knows what it is for?
Does the product automate what should be automated and require approval for what should be approved?
Is the product's value system (local-first, evidence-driven, consent-gated) visible?
Does the product record what should be recorded?
Can academic journal monitoring be automated through existing recipes?
Does the product's philosophy need a plain-language articulation for non-technical audiences?

floors.persona_domain: boss floor: AI self-inspection -- can a 70-year-old use this product independently?
questions: Does a simple mode exist that any elder could use on day one with zero technical help?
Can an elder with no technical background understand what this product does in 30 seconds?
If an elder gets confused at any point, is there a human they can contact?
Does the product pass WCAG 2.1 AA (contrast, keyboard nav, timing, target size, ARIA)?
If placed in a senior center and introduced by a volunteer, could elders use it after 30 minutes?
Does the product's visual design signal 'this was made for you' to an elder user?
If a simple mode were built, what percentage of the 47 elder floors would clear?

floors.persona_domain: boss floor: Tyson's grandmother, family trust test
questions: Can the text be made bigger and the background lighter?
Will the computer wait a few minutes for the user to read before clicking?
Can the product explain what it does the way you would explain it to a grandmother?
If something goes wrong, who does the user call?
Is the product going to share information with companies without asking?
Will this cost money the user does not expect?
The grandson trusts this. Should the grandmother?
